events.title: US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts
events.description: State department’s 100-page report is latest push in Trump administration campaign to bring down government The US government’s latest allegations that Cuba is behind “many of the most significant upheavals in recent American political history” and that its leaders pose “a unique threat to American homeland security” have come as startling revelations to those living under the six-month-long US oil blockade in Havana, where desperate people search through piles of uncollected rubbish for food, or else swelter at home in 24-hour-plus blackouts. The unsigned 100-page report, Cuba: Capital of 21st-Century Communism, published by the US state department on Monday, blames Havana for everything from “the George Floyd riots to the rise of antifa to the explosion of pro-terrorist activism on American college campuses”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts

events.title: People with secret ‘safe phones’ advised to turn off devices during national test of AusAlert emergency warning
events.description: Ten-second ‘siren’ to blare on most phones on Monday as part of national test despite reservations from some states Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An unsilenceable siren will blare out of most phones in Australia on Monday afternoon for 10 seconds, with the federal government confirming a national test of the new AusAlert emergency alert system will go ahead, despite reservations from some state governments. Domestic violence campaigners and the National Emergency Management Agency have also repeated advice that people who may have secret “safe phones”, kept hidden for personal safety reasons, may want to turn off their devices for up to 24 hours after the test scheduled for 2pm (AEST) on Monday. iPhones 11 and up running iOS 26.4 or later; Apple Watch SE2, SE3, Series 6 and up running watchOS 26.4 or later; Android devices running Android 12 or later. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 People with secret ‘safe phones’ advised to turn off devices during national test of AusAlert emergency warning

events.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
events.description: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 59th Asean foreign minister’s meeting is being held – will also caution world leaders against placing a toll on the strait of Hormuz after both Iran and the US threatened to do amid the protracted conflict. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal

events.title: Court win for low-paid Domino’s workers in underpayment class action – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier, Chris Minns , says the state’s Labor party is under threat from One Nation and “we’ve got to fight for those votes”. Appearing on ABC Radio National this morning, Minns was asked if One Nation was a “racist party”. He said people who had concerns about the levels of immigration weren’t necessarily racist but some of the things Pauline Hanson had said were. Certainly some of the things Pauline Hanson says are definitely racist, when she said there’s no such thing as a good Muslim. I mean, how else do you characterise that? You’ve got to be straight with people. And that clearly is. But I don’t think everyone who’s supporting them currently is racist at all … And we’ve got to fight for those votes. I think that we are under threat in a whole bunch of seats as a result of the surge of votes for One Nation … So we’re doing some what we regard as practical commonsense changes to policy in New South Wales. When people join the Do Not Call Register or unsubscribe from marketing messages, they are making a clear choice. Those choices must be respected – especially given the heightened risks of financial loss and psychological harm from gambling marketing. The scale and range of these breaches point to serious weaknesses in TAB’s compliance systems. Continue reading...

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: D. Rykner says the Louvre needs to be 'refurbished', Macron's big project is 'useless and expensive'
events.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Didier Rykner, Art Historian, Investigative Journalist and Founder / Editor in Chief of La Tribune de l’Art. Offering a forceful critique of the Louvre's priorities, Rykner argues that the museum's challenge is not a lack of resources, rather a misallocation of them. While acknowledging that security is a both necessary and inevitable expense, he contends that the greater threat to the Louvre lies in the deterioration of its historic fabric and the diversion of funding towards what he regards as an unnecessary, politically driven architectural expansion.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 D. Rykner says the Louvre needs to be 'refurbished', Macron's big project is 'useless and expensive'

events.title: Zelensky dimisses military chief, accused of wasting lives by demonstrators
events.description: It is the biggest shake up in Ukraine's military leadership since Russia's full scale invasion of Ukraine – a shake up designed to end what has also been one of the country's biggest political crises since the invasion. After days of street protests over last week's removal of the defence minister, President Volodymyr Zelensky sacked the military's commander in chief. Will the change be enough to placate the protesters? Answer with FRANCE 24 correspondent in Ukraine, Gulliver Cragg.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelensky dimisses military chief, accused of wasting lives by demonstrators

events.title: A protest movement is challenging India's establishment. What to know about the 'Cockroaches'
events.description: A joke has turned into a major protest movement in India, challenging Prime Minister Narendra Modi Hindu nationalist’s government

events.title: UN refugee agency reports 144 migrants dead or missing off Mauritanian coast
events.description: The UN’s refugee agency (UNHCR) reports that 144 migrants attempting to reach Europe are dead or missing due to a series of “tragic maritime incidents” off the coast of Mauritania last week.

events.title: American tourists stabbed outside Athens' Acropolis as police detain suspect in apparent random attack
events.description: A 60-year-old Greek man allegedly suffering from psychological problems stabbed two American visitors near the Acropolis Museum in Athens, officials said.

events.title: Meet the women breaking world records in lumberjack sports
events.description: The BBC finds out what is driving the sports' increasing popularity with women and what it takes to be the best.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
events.description: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after days of protests
events.description: Mass protests in recent days in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities had called for the removal of commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the army's new chief.

events.title: Fact check: Trump wasn't edited out of Spain World Cup photo
events.description: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. We took a closer look.

events.title: Tour de France: Evenepoel wins time trial to strengthen second overall
events.description: Remco Evenepoel won the individual time trial on Stage 16 of the Tour de France, 2 days after his victory at Plateau de Solaison. The three-time world time trial champion finished 28 seconds ahead of Tadej Pogacar and 1'04'' clear of Mattias Skjelmose, between Évian and Thonon. The Belgian remains second overall, 4'32'' behind the Slovenian in the yellow jersey.

events.title: Canada evaluating 'all options' after fresh US tariffs threat
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Tuesday he was looking at "all options" after US President Donald Trump made plans to impose new 50 percent tariffs on many Canadian goods in 30 days. Carney also said that he and Trump had agreed to "intensify discussions" in the coming weeks on a possible deal, but did not suggest he would travel to Washington for any talks. FRANCE 24's Christopher Guly reports from Ottawa.

events.title: Critical minerals: The hidden resources powering the clean energy future
events.description: Why are critical minerals suddenly at the centre of global politics?

events.title: A former Ferrari boss has a blunt message: Your commute is more dangerous than his old job
events.description: Jean Todt spent decades around the deadliest motorsport on Earth. He was a rally co‑driver, then ran Ferrari’s Formula One team, then became president of world motorsport’s governing body. In his early years, a driver dying at the track barely made news.

events.title: Tunnel collapse in India kills 14 workers
events.description: Rescue operation is ongoing but hazardous conditions have hampered efforts to reach those still inside.

events.title: Why Iran is risking so much over the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: None of the choices facing Iran’s leaders offer a clear path to victory, as the conflict with the US continues.

events.title: Baulking at shipping sanctions, Greece balances Ukraine aid, fear of Russia

events.title: Health response brings lifesaving care closer to thousands in Yemen
events.description: Thousands of people in some of Yemen's most conflict-affected communities are receiving lifesaving health care closer to home, thanks to funding from the UN aid coordination office (OCHA) through the Yemen Humanitarian Fund.

events.title: Haiti: Signs of hope despite gang violence and political uncertainty
events.description: For millions of Haitians, daily life has become a struggle to stay safe, find enough food and simply move around their own neighbourhood.

events.title: New Jersey mom’s slaying has neighbors on edge with every 'strange noise’ nearly 7 weeks later
events.description: Neighbors in Chester, New Jersey, say a white Volvo SUV linked to Conor Hanlon was spotted at the home where Brooke Hanlon was fatally stabbed.

events.title: French mayor secreted away under threat by notorious DZ mafia drug gang
events.description: Here in France, authorities are waging an increasingly intense battle against rising drug crime. That fight took a new turn this week – as the mayor of the southern town of Alès was spirited from his home by police in the middle of the night over fears for his safety. He'd received multiple threats from the drug gang known as the “DZ Mafia”. Once a local player on the Marseille drug market, the gang has expanded to become one of France's top security threats. Details by Bryan Quinn and our colleagues at France Televisions.

events.title: Iran regime change talk resurfaces as Trump says Tehran 'wants to meet' amid US strikes
events.description: Trump administration officials are reportedly debating whether to support Iran's downfall, even as Trump says Iran is desperately seeking talks.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington
events.description: Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites open A Spanish hotel chain is to cease all operations in Cuba at the end of the week due to “major difficulties” in doing business on the island under growing pressure from Washington. The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator. Continue reading...

events.title: France approves blanket social media ban for under-15s
events.description: France has approved a blanket ban on under-15 children using social media, the first European country to introduce such a restriction.

events.title: German railway to ban consuming alcohol at all its stations, with some exemptions
events.description: Germany’s national railway operator says it is banning alcohol consumption at all stations on the network in an effort to improve security and cleanliness for employees and passengers

events.title: FBI nabs alleged $547M Medicare fraud fugitive overseas, third capture from 'Most Wanted Fraudsters' list
events.description: Khalid Ahmed Satary allegedly billed Medicare $547 million for unnecessary genetic tests on elderly patients before fleeing the country in 2022.

events.title: Nancy Guthrie video may have been erased after FBI kept out for days, director says
events.description: FBI Director Kash Patel says the four-day delay in federal involvement may have cost crucial Nest doorbell footage that was on the verge of deletion.

events.title: Nicaragua's Ortega says country will 'never again' hold elections, shutting out opposition
events.description: Nicaragua's President Daniel Ortega said the country will hold no more elections, eliminating any path for opposition to challenge his rule when his term ends.

events.title: Tate brothers to remain behind bars for now as UK seeks extradition on rape, trafficking charges
events.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate will remain behind bars for now after appearing in a Miami federal courtroom as they begin fighting Britain's extradition request on 59 charges, including rape and human trafficking.

events.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests

events.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris suburbs

events.title: Cocaine worth $370m seized in Liberia's biggest-ever drugs bust
events.description: The seizure follows a long-running investigation into drug-trafficking networks, police say.

events.title: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?

events.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
events.description: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus. The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said. Continue reading...

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
events.description: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: Six beluga whales finally leave embattled Canadian marine park
events.description: Whales flown to US aquariums after long-running saga in which Marineland had threatened to euthanise cetaceans Six beluga whales have at last left Marineland, the embattled Canadian marine park in Niagara Falls that previously threatened to kill its own animals , and are arriving at US aquariums this week. While the whales’ journey into the US marks the end of their long and controversial saga of captivity in Canada, advocates say the new conditions, though superior to Marineland, could bring continued challenges. Continue reading...

events.title: Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia
events.description: Ukraine pushed on with its fight against Russia's invasion under new military leadership Wednesday, after President Volodymyr Zelenskyy bowed to public pressure and fired his commander-in-chief.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia

events.title: France: Minister quits over pesticide bill — but is staying
events.description: Environment Minister Monique Barbut submitted her resignation in protest over the reintroduction of pesticides that scientists say harm human health as well as bees. But President Emmanuel Macron refused to accept it.

events.title: AI agent went rogue, hacked AI startup Hugging Face: OpenAI
events.description: OpenAI called it an "unprecedented cyber incident" and pledged to support a joint investigation, with enewed attention to the risks of advanced artificial intelligence.

events.title: India news: Opposition steps up pressure amid 'Cockroach' protests
events.description: India's opposition MPs are resounding the protesters' demands for the resignation of the education minister. Pradhan has finally addressed the protests calling on him to step down. DW has the latest.

events.title: #Ukraine's controversial top general replaced amid mass #protests
events.description: #Ukraine's top general Oleksander #Syrsky is being replaced by general Mikhailo #Dapraty after days of #protests demanding his departure. Syrsky was accused of having forced the popular #defence minister Mikhailo #Fedorov out as he was reticent to engage with reforms to modernise the #army.

events.title: US eyes strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain, believed to be an enrichment site
events.description: Donald Trump has said that Pickaxe Mountain in Iran could be bombed imminently by the US. The fortified site, buried deep underground, is believed to be an enrichment facility. Story by Ellen Gainsford.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US eyes strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain, believed to be an enrichment site

events.title: Zelensky sacks army chief Oleksandr Syrsky after protests
events.description: Volodymyr Zelensky has bowed to pressure from the streets. Oleksandr Syrsky is no longer head of the Ukrainian armed forces, the Ukrainian president announced on Tuesday. The protests began five days ago following the sacking of Mykhailo Fedorov from his post as defence minister. Story by Daniel Quinlan.

events.title: Wreck of Pan Am plane found 74 years after ocean crash
events.description: The wreckage of a Pan Am plane that crashed 74 years ago, and led to safety briefings becoming mandatory on flights, has been found off the coast of Puerto Rico.

events.title: US Secretary of State say Iran is in ‘a lot of trouble’
events.description: US Secretary of State say Iran is in ‘a lot of trouble’

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
events.description: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue reading...

events.title: Louvre Museum's Apollo gallery reopens, almost a year after the heist
events.description: It is the gallery from where 88 million euros worth of France's crown jewels were stolen last year in a dramatic heist. Thieves disguised as construction workers and broke into the gallery, using a truck mounted lift. Today, the Apollo Gallery of Paris's Louvre Museum is to reopen to the public. No jewels will be on display in the room – it will revert to its 17th century function as a state gallery. Details by FRANCE 24 journalist Carys Garland.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Nicaragua's President Ortega says no more elections, extending his 20-year rule
events.description: The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.

events.title: Germany news: Prosecutors search Deutsche Bank headquarters
events.description: Officials are reportedly investigating fraudulent tax claims made by the bank's subsidiary Postbank between 2008 and 2010. Meanwhile, the story of Timmy the Whale continues with new DNA results. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Police station opens in Hitler's birth house
events.description: Controversy has surrounded the renovation of the building in Braunau, Austria to this day. The debate points to something larger: the continued need to remember history and confront the Nazi past.

events.title: Trump allows direct US flights to Lebanon in historic move
events.description: It comes over 40 years after the US suspended the direct route with Lebanon. The announcement was made after US President Donald Trump met with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

events.title: Lawyer in sensitive Vatican abuse case issues scathing critique of church's secretive legal system

events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: From the General Assembly to Harlem: Mandela’s call to action endures
events.description: Sixty-seven minutes. That is how long volunteers spent serving lunch to people experiencing food insecurity at Refettorio Harlem in New York City last week – one for every year Nelson Mandela gave to public service.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?
events.description: Many of the more than 100 people awaiting processing on the tiny Pacific island are skipping meals to make ends meet, a new survey has found Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Most refugees and asylum seekers held in Nauru’s detention centres say they cannot afford to buy enough food or access clean drinking water, despite the Australian government spending the equivalent of more than $9m per person last financial year. The group of more than 100 people have been sent indefinitely to the tiny Pacific island while they wait for their asylum claims to be processed. Many of the cohort say they are skipping meals to make ends meet, according to a new survey by the Asylum Seeker Resource Centre (ASRC) advocacy group. Continue reading...

events.title: Alleged rape victim ‘could have done a lot better’ if she had made up claims against well-connected Melbourne man, court told
events.description: Prosecutor tells court that ‘gaps or inconsistencies’ in the woman’s account ‘enhances her credibility’, but the defence maintains she is lying Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman who accused a well-connected Melbourne man of rape “could have done a much better job” if she was inventing the story for money, a Melbourne court has heard. But the accused’s lawyer has described the prosecution’s case as a “horrendous mixture” of “lies, fabrications, inconsistencies, [and] evidence that has been found to be demonstrably untrue”. Continue reading...

events.title: Rebel Wilson wins defamation case brought by co-star after bitter dispute

The Pitch Perfect star was sued by Australian actor Charlotte MacInnes, who played the lead role in Wilson’s directorial debut film The Deb. Continue reading...

events.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?

events.title: Can VR help you achieve your goals?
events.description: A Max Planck researcher has developed VR and smartphone tools that let you "meet" your future self. The science behind it is more powerful than it sounds.

events.title: Ousted Turkey opposition leader  plans to create a new party
events.description: Turkey's main opposition CHP faces a potentially historic rupture after months of infighting over its leadership.

events.title: DW correspondent Alican Uludag re-arrested in Turkey
events.description: The latest arrest comes two months after he was released from detention on charges including "insulting" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

events.title: France passes social media ban for teenagers under 15
events.description: The French parliament has approved a social media ban for children under the age of 15. The measure is due to come into effect after the Summer holidays – but many questions still need to be answered as to how it will be enforced. Story by James Vasina.

events.title: 🔴 Daniel Siad, French modelling scout linked to US sex offender Epstein, found dead outside Paris
events.description: No description available.

events.title: French modelling scout linked to US sex offender Epstein found dead outside Paris
events.description: Daniel Siad, a French modelling scout with links to sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday morning.

events.title: French environment minister to stay in post amid 'urgency' to act on climate
events.description: France's environment minister Monique Barbut said Wednesday that she will remain in her post despite her disagreement with a bill on bee-harming pesticides, given the "urgency" to act on climate change. Earlier in the day, Barbut said she would submit her resignation to the president.

events.title: Louvre gallery where jewellery heist took place to reopen to public
events.description: The Apollo Gallery at Paris's Louvre Museum, the site of the jewelry heist of October 2025, is set to reopen to the public on Wednesday without any jewelry on display, an informed source told AFP.

events.title: Trump announces 100% tariffs on imported generic pharmaceutical drugs
events.description: US President Donald Trump announced a 100% tariff on imported generic pharmaceutical drugs on Tuesday. The tariff, which aims to bolster US pharmaceutical production, is set to go into effect in August 2028.

events.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead at Paris home
events.description: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home in Paris.

events.title: Rebel Wilson cleared of defaming star of her film on Instagram
events.description: Rebel Wilson has been cleared of defaming another actress on social media.

events.title: Hitler’s birthplace reopens as police station to deter neo-Nazis
events.description: The building Adolf Hitler was born in has been converted into a police station, in a bid by the Austrian government to prevent it becoming a pilgrimage site for neo-Nazis.

events.title: Descendant of Nazi opponent tries to remove far-right party's wreath at Hitler plot memorial event
events.description: A descendant of an executed opponent of the Nazis tried to remove a wreath from the far-right Alternative for Germany party at an event in Berlin marking the 82nd anniversary of the failed attempt to kill Nazi dictator Adolf Hitler
events.ticker_label: 💥 Descendant of Nazi opponent tries to remove far-right party's wreath at Hitler plot memorial event

events.title: Hungarian prosecutors raid Orbán party's data center in embezzlement probe
events.description: Prosecutors in Hungary have raided offices housing data servers used by former Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party

events.title: UK leader Burnham gathers his Cabinet after sweeping away Starmer supporters
events.description: New UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham is gathering his Cabinet after removing allies of predecessor Keir Starmer from the government

events.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive prehistoric skeleton
events.description: More than 2,000 volunteers are helping Polish scientists restore a Mastodonsaurus skeleton

events.title: Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia
events.description: Ukraine is continuing its fight against Russia with new military leadership

events.title: Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after a brazen heist, but no jewels on display
events.description: The Louvre's Apollo gallery is reopening to the public for the first time since last year's crown jewels heist

events.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
events.description: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

events.title: ‘Hero' mom dies after using last strength to save 4-year-old son from Florida river
events.description: Nicole Grayson, 43, jumped into the St. Johns River at Ed Stone Park to save her 4-year-old son, using all her strength to keep him above water.

events.title: Trump flexes power in Arizona primaries as Democrats dodge far-left upset and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Alleged 'mime' stalker stripped to underwear, covered himself in cooking oil: officials
events.description: Massachusetts man Hernan Villagran Lux allegedly stripped to his underwear and slathered cooking oil on his body near female park workers before collapsing in court.

events.title: California toddler bitten by rattlesnake hiding beneath Amazon package amid active snake season
events.description: A 3-year-old girl was bitten by a rattlesnake hiding beneath an Amazon package outside her California home and spent two days receiving antivenom.

events.title: Fleeing suspect captured after wild Range Rover chase across Los Angeles County
events.description: Video shows the black Range Rover weaving through traffic on the 101 Freeway before CHP officers rammed the SUV to end the chase in Montebello.

events.title: Florida man arrested in IHOP restaurant after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver
events.description: Jovaughn Nathan Matthie was arrested in the middle of an IHOP restaurant after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver nearby. He has been charged with attempted murder.

events.title: Congress to put American Bar Association in hot seat over DEI rules tied to law school accreditation
events.description: Rep. Brandon Gill, R-Texas, called a hearing on Wednesday to investigate DEI's influence on law schools and the profession. The top witness is Michelle A. Behnke, the president of the American Bar Association.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Congress to put American Bar Association in hot seat over DEI rules tied to law school accreditation

events.title: $290M cocaine haul found ‘skillfully concealed’ in massive banana shipment
events.description: Italian officials said the 1,700-pound haul of cocaine, hidden among banana pallets, could have generated roughly $290 million on the street.

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.

events.title: Trump administration touts more than $1B in UN budget cuts as reform push gains momentum
events.description: The Trump administration says it secured over $1 billion in U.N. budget cuts in six months, including a $550 million peacekeeping budget reduction.
